chapter_title.en: Chapter 15 – Making the People of the Book to swear (an oath)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/335)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/335)
text: لَا يُحْلَفْ بِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ وَ قَالَ الْيَهُودِيُّ وَ النَّصْرَانِيُّ وَ الْمَجُوسِيُّ لَا تُحْلِفُوهُمْ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ .

en.hubeali: From him, from Al Nazar Bin Suweyd, from Al Qasim Bin Suleyman, from Jarrah Al Madainy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Do not get (anyone) to swear without Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Jews, and the Christians, and the Magians, do not get them to swear except by Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic’.
en.sarwar: 5. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from al-Qasim ibn Sulayman from Jarrah al- Mada’iniy who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘An oath is not taken by anything other than by Allah.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘In the case of Jews, Christians and Zoroastrian, you must not make them to take an oath by anything other than by Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious.””
